item.title: Here's how the U.S.-Iran war is already hitting consumers' pocketbooks
item.description: The U.S.-Israeli strikes on Iran over the weekend gave way to a week with topsy-turvy markets, spiking mortgage rates and higher prices at the pump.

item.title: U.S. payrolls unexpectedly fell by 92,000 in February; unemployment rate rises to 4.4%
item.description: Nonfarm payrolls were expected to increase 50,000 in February while the unemployment rate held steady at 4.3%.

item.title: San Francisco Fed's Daly says jobs report complicates interest rate call
item.description: Daly told CNBC on Friday that the weak February jobs report adds to a difficult policymaking environment.

item.title: UAE mulls freezing Iranian assets as Middle East conflict escalates: WSJ
item.description: The UAE is considering freezing billions of dollars of Iranian assets held in the Gulf state, potentially severing one of Tehran's most critical econo...

item.title: The February jobs numbers are coming out Friday. Here's what to expect
item.description: Economists surveyed by Dow Jones expect payroll growth of 50,000, following January's surprisingly high 130,000.
